analysis_framework: Who, What, When, Where, Why, How

primary_stakeholders.description: Build and maintain skills for hierarchical collaboration diagrams with boundary concepts
Use enhanced skills to decompose complex organizational processes into hierarchical boundaries
Ensure generated artifacts comply with evolutionary development principles

secondary_stakeholders.description: Existing users whose workflows must remain unbroken
Responsible for evolving organizational models with new hierarchical concepts
Validate boundary detection accuracy, decomposition rule enforcement, EDPS compliance

core_capabilities.description: Extend diagram-generatecollaboration with Mermaid box syntax, participant stereotypes, and decomposition rules
Enable control-type interactions to become sub-processes with auto-generated folder structures
Complexity metrics, decomposition suggestions, and detail level guidelines per hierarchy level
Validate evolutionary development principles, change impact analysis, requirements traceability
Update organizational models with boundary concepts, hierarchical diagrams, and templates
Import Project 1 diagrams, provide migration tools, preserve existing links

nonfunctional_requirements.integration: Seamless VS Code and GitHub Copilot integration

milestones.description: Full hierarchy management with sub-folders and navigation
EDPS compliance validation passing all tests
Migration complete, OrgModel updated, documentation delivered

technical_environment.diagram_rendering: Mermaid sequence diagrams with box syntax
technical_environment.document_format: Markdown with embedded Mermaid and JSON metadata

why.problem_statement: Project 1 skills produce single-level flat collaboration diagrams that cannot represent complex multi-component systems, forcing oversized unreadable diagrams or loss of detail, violating EDPS methodology requirements for evolutionary process decomposition.
business_value: Complexity management through navigable process hierarchies
EDPS methodology alignment fulfilling organizational commitment
Scalable modeling handling unlimited system complexity
Structured knowledge organization through folder hierarchies
Change impact analysis across process levels
Organizational model evolution reflecting methodology capabilities

implementation_strategy: Create dedicated new skills for hierarchy management and boundary validation
Use participant stereotypes as central organizing principle
Map process hierarchy directly to folder structure
Provide optional migration path for Project 1 diagrams

key_technical_decisions.rationale: Only viable option for grouping in Mermaid sequence diagrams
